beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A practical order runs from Claude For Dummies through basics, Cowork, Skills, and voice, certification, and de-AI-ing. Guides go stale fast because Claude keeps shipping, so old prompting advice actively misleads you. Popularity is not currency: widely shared instructions become harmful once the interface and model change. Add the vibecoding guide, save the list, and block twenty minutes this week for just the first one.

props.artifactTitle: Claude reading order that stays current
artifactLines: A practical order runs from Claude For Dummies through basics, Cowork, Skills, and voice, certification, and de-AI-ing.
Guides go stale fast because Claude keeps shipping, so old prompting advice actively misleads you.
Popularity is not currency: widely shared instructions become harmful once the interface and model change.
Add the vibecoding guide, save the list, and block twenty minutes this week for just the first one.

beats.narration_text: The idea that a popular guide written in January is actively wrong by March, because the model behavior changed, is what I want to defend against. I'd rather build a habit than memorize stale steps. Help me (1) turn the seven-guide reading order into a realistic one-per-day week starting with Claude For Dummies, (2) write a quick test I can apply to any Claude tip to decide if it's obsolete, and (3) explain why vibecoding is the concept most worth learning even though its specific UI steps age fastest.

props.command: The idea that a popular guide written in January is actively wrong by March, because the model behavior changed, is what I want to defend against. I'd rather build a habit than memorize stale steps. Help me (1) turn the seven-guide reading order into a realistic one-per-day week starting with Claude For Dummies, (2) write a quick test I can apply to any Claude tip to decide if it's obsolete, and (3) explain why vibecoding is the concept most worth learning even though its specific UI steps age fastest.

beats.narration_text: Here is a practical reading order — seven guides, each with a time estimate so you can slot them into a week. Claude For Dummies, five minutes: the absolute starting point if you've never opened Claude. Claude basics, nine to ten minutes: what you can actually do with it. Claude Cowork, twenty minutes — the core guide to the multiagent mode for non-coders. Claude Skills, fifteen minutes: how to make any recurring task repeatable. Then three more: how to train Claude on your voice, how to get certified for free, and how to stop sounding like AI.

artifactLines: #1 Claude For Dummies — 5 min. Never tried Claude? Start here.
#2 Claude basics — 9-10 min. What Claude can actually do.
#3 Claude Cowork — 20 min. Multiagent work for non-coders.
#4-7: Skills (15 min), Voice training, Free certification, Anti-AI writing.

beats.narration_text: Why are twenty-one guides outdated? Because Claude itself keeps shipping. A guide written about Cowork in January is wrong by March. The model that was smartest in February — Opus 4.6 — is now behind 4.7, which works differently enough that old prompting advice actively misleads you. Popularity is not currency: even widely shared instructions become harmful when the interface and model behavior change. Treat stale procedural content as obsolete.

props.artifactTitle: Why the old guides are wrong
artifactLines: Claude ships new models every few months. Guides written for 4.6 are wrong on 4.7.
Cowork's interface changed — the folder setup that worked in January breaks in March.
Even the most-shared procedural guides can become obsolete.
The Claude 101 syllabus = the seven that are still accurate as of today.

beats.narration_text: One additional guide belongs beside the official seven: the Claude Code newsletter, on vibecoding: describing what you want while Claude Code implements it — a website, hosted, with real functionality, zero coding knowledge required. Vibecoding is describing what you want in plain English and letting Claude write every line of code. Specific UI instructions age quickly, but the concept is the fastest-moving part of the Claude ecosystem right now.

beats.narration_text: The practical next step is simple. Save the guide. Block twenty minutes this week — don't read all seven right now, just the first one. Send it to anyone who still hasn't tried Claude or thinks it's a person's name. That last bit is a recurring joke, but it's also a real distribution strategy: the newsletter grows from shares, not ads. Every guide ends the same way because it works. And the strategy itself is a lesson about building with AI — show the work, make it repeatable, let people forward it.

artifactLines: Save the guide. Don't read all seven right now — pick one this week.
Block 20 minutes. Calendar it. Otherwise it doesn't happen.
Start with #1 (Claude For Dummies) if you've never run a Claude session.
Start with #3 (Cowork) if you already know the basics.

beats.narration_text: Here's the honest verdict on This seven-guide syllabus. It's genuinely useful — but only if you treat it as a living document, not a finished canon. Claude ships fast enough that any guide older than a few months needs a mental asterisk. The ones on the list that age best are the conceptual ones: how Cowork works, what Skills are for, why you sound like AI. The ones that age fastest are anything touching the UI or model selectors, because those interfaces keep changing. Use the list to orient — then update as you go.

props.artifactTitle: Which guides age well vs. badly
artifactLines: Ages well: Cowork concepts, Skills logic, voice training, anti-AI writing.
Ages fast: anything with UI screenshots or model-selector steps.
Rule: conceptual guides last. Setup guides go stale with every Claude release.
Treat the list as orientation — not gospel. Update your mental model as Claude ships.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Tell Claude which guide from the seven you just read and ask it to walk you through the hands-on setup in real time.

props.command: I just learned the basics of [Cowork / Skills / Claude]. Walk me through the setup hands-on. Ask me what I've already done so we start in the right place.
